title: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken

seo.title: How to Make Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken — Easy American Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken. This easy American recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken
easy Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken recipe
authentic American Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken
lowCompetitionKeywords: American Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken Recipe
h2Headings: What is Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic American Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken — a beloved American classic

description: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken is the epitome of comfort food, featuring tender, juicy chicken encased in a perfectly crispy, golden-brown crust. Marinated in buttermilk, it gains incredible flavor and moisture, making every bite a delightful experience. This classic American dish is a true celebration of Southern culinary tradition.

ingredients.notes: bone-in, skin-on, cut into serving pieces
freshly ground, plus more for seasoning

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the buttermilk and hot sauce (if using). Season the chicken pieces generously with salt and black pepper. Submerge the seasoned chicken in the buttermilk mixture, ensuring all pieces are well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, to allow the chicken to tenderize and absorb flavor.

instructions.title: Set Up the Dredging Station
instructions.text: In a shallow dish or large resealable bag, whisk together the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cayenne pepper, dried thyme, 2 teaspoons of salt, and 1 teaspoon of black pepper. Mix thoroughly to combine all the spices evenly.

instructions.title: Dredge the Chicken
instructions.text: Remove the chicken pieces from the buttermilk, allowing any excess to drip off but do not rinse. One piece at a time, dredge the chicken in the seasoned flour mixture, pressing firmly to ensure a thick, even coating. Place the dredged chicken on a wire rack set over a baking sheet and let it rest for 15-20 minutes. This helps the coating adhere better.

instructions.title: Heat the Frying Oil
instructions.text: In a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, pour enough vegetable or peanut oil to reach a depth of about 3 inches. Heat the oil over medium-high heat until it reaches 325-350°F (160-175°C). Use a thermometer to monitor the temperature for best results.

instructions.title: Fry the Chicken in Batches
instructions.text: Carefully lower 3-4 pieces of chicken into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry the chicken for about 6-8 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until golden brown and cooked through. Larger pieces like breasts or thighs may take longer. Maintain a consistent oil temperature.

instructions.title: Check for Doneness and Drain
instructions.text: The chicken is done when its internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at the thickest part. Once cooked, remove the chicken from the oil and place it on a clean wire rack set over paper towels to drain excess oil. Season immediately with a pinch of salt. Repeat with remaining chicken batches, ensuring the oil returns to temperature between batches.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Allow the fried chicken to rest for a few minutes before serving. This helps the juices redistribute, resulting in more tender and flavorful chicken. Serve hot with your favorite Southern sides like mashed potatoes, coleslaw, or biscuits.

tips: For the crispiest crust, allow the dredged chicken to rest for at least 15-20 minutes before frying. This 'sets' the coating.
If you don't have buttermilk, you can make a substitute by adding 1 tablespoon of white vinegar or lemon juice to 1 cup of regular milk and letting it sit for 5-10 minutes.
To keep fried chicken warm and crispy while cooking batches, place it on a wire rack in a preheated oven at 200°F (95°C).
article: Southern Buttermilk Fried Chicken is a beloved American Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across American. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
